RAILWAY DILL


b[F0R[ 5[hAT[
Adoption of the Majority
Report is Urged by


Senator Baker.
TRYING TO HURRY VOTE
Railway MN Anxious to Dispose
of Measure Before Action is


Taken in House.

TRYING TO HURRY VOTE

pages.raw_text:
OLYMPIA, Wash, Feb. 1.-THE
senate took up the committee report
on the railroad commission bill at
II o'clock this morning. 1t was call
ed up as a special order with a divid-
ed committee report. Senator Baker
Immediately moved the adoption of
the majority report, which recon-
mends indefinite postponement of the
measure.


Senator Tolman moved a substitute
of the minority report. In the dis
cussion of the substitute report Sena-
tors Tolman. Graves, Grow, Hallet,
CARRIER and Warburton spoke for the
measure, while Senator Hamilton
spoke against and for the majority
report.


The railroad men are trying to
hurry the measure to a vote before
the house takes action.


If it is discovered that the senate
debate is going to be long drawn out,
it Is believed a sufficient number of
members in the house will discover
that the regular calendar demands i'm
mediate attention, and this will delay
consideration of the commission bill
until tomorrow.
